item.title: Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK? — 37 replies — 148.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK?

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-07-15 08:27 UTC

OP

Every time I see the UK mentioned here, it is always in a negative light.

It is always
>In the USA, wages are better!
>In Europe, houses are cheaper!
>The FTSE is underperforming!
>My taxes are too high!

There is never any mention of the fantastic benefits which come with life in the UK. The fantastic healthcare for minimal cost, the great education system available to everybody, the dynamic modern economy with many bustling sectors, and everything else.

Taxes are higher than the USA but they are lower than most of Europe and we still enjoy fantastic public services whereas in America there are no public services or safety nets.

Not to mention the things you cannot measure. Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs. Enjoying the fantastic beautiful nature and gardens. Living in the historical majesty and balancing that with modern cosmopolitan qualities.

The UK might not be the best place to become rich but nobody is poor here and it is very easy to enjoy life if you have goals beyond the number in your bank account.

Early replies (first 3)

62481973
>>62481958
the problem is that its being filled with immigrants who are living off tax payers money
thats the gist of why everyone is so pissed off here

62481986
>>62481973
So just right wing racebaiting?

No, I think it runs deeper than that. Most people have not personally had negative experiences with migrants, and reading the Daily Mail does not count.

I am trying to angle from a financial perspective too. People talk about the UK like it is a poor country but it is one of the richest countries on the planet. I think perhaps they are just spoiled and do not know what poor countries are really like. I blame social media.

62481994
>>62481958
>fantastic healthcare for minimal cost
my nan lived in birmingham and she came down with leukaemia and was gone within a couple of months. She never even got to see a doctor because the muslim immigrants were getting seen first. if i had something to lose i wouldnt even dare post this for fear of legal reprimand.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62482008
>>62481986
Didnt it recently make news as its as poor as the poorest US state (Mississippi)

62482009
>>62482001
na mate i think brits who have paid taxes their entire lives ought to get treated before migrants.

62482013
>>62482009
Police are on their way

62482014
>>62481986
Yeah the UK definitely isnt poor, but its bankrupting itself. Especially with the refusal to drill and accumulate the assets we have underground, we have fucking shit tons of oil but we wont drill we just buy it from other countries and anything we do drill we sell it to get processed in another country then we buy it back, fucking insanity. Norway know what they are doing in regards to oil and gas

62482025
>>62482008
GDP is not a very good metric for this. There are lots of things which complicate the issue.

You cannot directly compare life in a quiet quaint English village to a tropical American suburb where you need to drive hours to even buy groceries and rely on AC to cool your wooden house on the basis of... how much money companies are making?

I know where I would rather live.

62482033
>>62481958
>The fantastic healthcare for minimal cost, the great education system available to everybody, the dynamic modern economy with many bustling sectors, and everything else
>Not to mention the things you cannot measure. Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs.

I dont know if this is bait or genuine.

62482036
>>62482033
Assume bait, based on the other comments

62482043
>>62482033
Because you and I think most of the naysayers, have never actually been to the UK, have you?

62482100
>>62481986
Go back (to reddit)

62482110
>>62481986
>racebaiting
My negro, indigenous British births fall below 50% next year. Pull yourself out of the Marxist stupor.

62482113
>You should be okay becoming a minority in your own country because... racism or something
People like OP actually think like this

62482125
>>62481958
Have you ever thought that maybe its because it sucks
Have you ever thought that you were getting fed lies by your government who want you to not flee your backwards, devolving country for a better life?

62482131
>>62482113
Ive talked with multiple brits and part of the reason why their country is dying is they genuinely believe it.

62482136
>>62482131
It's incontrovertible - on the current trajectory Brits will be a minority by 2055. From 90% British to a minority in less than one lifetime.

62482137
>>62481958
Blatant gov shill post lmao

62482141
>>62481994
Yep it is shit and people die all the time due to strained past breaking care. Even this evening I was talking about a close relation who died at 36 because they missed years worth of opportunities to catch his cancer including straight up losing a biopsy and rebooking another 4 months later by which point he suffered pathological spinal fractures during the attempt to get the second.

62482147
>>62481958
Are the sandwhich meal deals that good

62482152
>>62482141
Actually writing this shit gets me so mad/upset. If we had been in basically any other first world nation he would still be here. But the UK set itself on a death spiral where the dark eyes brown golems of the world come for free money, healthcare and rape. Meanwhile all our services suffer, we get paid fuck all as the economy collapses and then the gov takes half of that again. Im so fucking done with this shit.

62482157
>>62481958
Wages are ass and the standard of living is far far lower. Just take a look at British houses and it's easy to see them lacking the basics Americans enjoy. They'll be tiny, dingy places with low ceilings and no AC, tiny ass fridges, no ice machines, no BBQs, shitty dishwashers, no dedicated laundry room, shitty to basically nonfunctional dryer, no garbage disposal, etc etc. Even then all the shit will be more expensive than the US.

>The UK might not be the best place to become rich but nobody is poor here and it is very easy to enjoy life if you have goals beyond the number in your bank account.

The US is in decline because we decided to value personal freedom over responsibilities such as education, accepting help, helping others, getting out of the way if you're stupid.

since we can't infringe on the rights of stupid people and insist on letting them run the country we're dropping fast. However those same stupid people are completely convinced the US is the best place ever. Mostly because they've never been anywhere else and aren't smart enough or humble enough to accept how other people do better at lots of things.

so yeah, idiots that are super proud of themselves. We let them vote when we should be locking them in a dark factory making toothbrushes or something.

62483238

>only £1.4k positive from whites

62483304

it's a woke version of north korea that's rapidly turning into the third world. in a few decades, actual brits living there will be a persecuted minority, like in south africa, you stupid re-dditor.

>>62477138

62477191
>>62477152

62477445
>>62477152
>You didn't say "miners don't want to sell at a loss", you said "miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC".

62477487
>>62477445
If you think those are the same thing I can see why you are frustrated with someone pointing out the error to begin with.

The market decides the price. Miners have 2 options:

1. Sell now to cover costs
2. Eat the costs and wait for the market to improve

Neither scenario is them setting the price. Scenario 2 is miners speculating with coins the same way any market participant does, except their tokens were previously uncirculating.

62479611
>>62477445
Those two statements are WORLDS apart.

You are seriously stupid, I want you to know that so maybe in the future you won't post as much out of embarrassment (slim chance, I know).

62481334
>>62477487
Dude, the BTC network CANNOT be sustainable if the price of BTC doesn't factor in the cost of maintain the network. No market in existence is mutually exclusive from the costs of operations to operate the economy the market is built on top of. You have to have some counter force to weaken its force to manipulate it in your favor.

Miners are the ONLY ones injecting new BTC into the network. I'm not saying they are the sole arbiters that dictate the price of BTC (you have to as retarded as >>62479611 to interpret it this way else miners would shoot it up to $1M a BTC already) but they sure as hell can influence the price purely by their position. This is why the inflation comparison between L1s, BTC notably, and Chainlink are flawed. New link enter the market through Node operators selling and also CLL selling. More damningly, the payments made to CLL to access the services provided by the node operators aren't in Link nor are they converted in am manner to transparently tie the link to the network usage by having the revenue and subsequently market pressure buy be a representation of usage. 62479611 can't refute this thus he has to resort to ad hominem attacks and making mountains out of molehills like the cocksucker he is.

62481363
>>62477191

62481409
>>62481334

>miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC
to
>miners can influence the price of BTC

These two statements are once again worlds apart.
And the only way miners can actually influence the price of BTC is negatively: the less they sell the less they negatively impact the price.

Just eat the fucking L you sperg.

Literally all crypto functions this way and resistance bands for each crypto individually establish in interconnected ways for this reason. You are literally just looking at one of them and being like "see see!!!! Look, mine is special!" and ignoring all the others. You aren't noticing anything anyone is missing, you are REALLY retardedly hyper-fixating on one and missing the cross-market theme and thinking it is longterm edge despite no mechanism for actual price growth or even sustenance being built just...cuz. item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 212 replies — 121.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 212 • Updated: 2026-07-15 06:48 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62474626
>>62451403
im in the same boat as you OP. start using your local resources. you said you live in the woods? start selling firewood. grow a bunch of plants and start a nursery and start selling plants. designer plants for decoration like japanese maples and shit. its a good side gig. make a website and shit for it then in a year or two when you apply for a real job say you were an entreprenuer and sold your business and want the security net of a regular 9-5 job now with the shitty economy. you can also just backdate this shit and fabricate a website and reviews for something that never happened too btw if you want to just start applying now withou getting any extra money or learning how business works

62474732
>>62450440
They don’t hire anyone retard

62474735
>>62452288
They
Don’t
Hire
You

62474751
even though I'm not a neet after being laid off I feel like I'm in a similar situation.
I have a leg up with my work experience and education but I don't feel like it was worth it which meant that I wasted my youth doing something even worse than just fucking around

62474795
to all the people in this situation who want actual sane advice, here it:

Do something easy. Something that won't demand a superior work ethic or a miserable grind with a manager looking at production numbers watching you.
You're likely gonna hate your life every single morning you wake up and have to work AND you didn't do this when you were 20 years old and it was easy. You can get over the hump and become a "worker" but the mornings are already your enemy, don't stack the odds further against you.

Work behind a register. Ideally at a gas station or some sleepy business. Maybe a long shot for you to get hired, but it's ideal. Some cleaning, some dealing with dickheads, some stocking. You won't be worked to the bone. A grocery/dollar store cashier is worse in most cases.

Inbound call centre. Yes you will hate your job but the work comes at you and your office should be air conditioned. If you have superior (compared to your coworkers) manners and people skills you may even find yourself promoted rapidly. 60 year old grannies get jobs in call centers and end up trainers and managers, you can do it.

Janitor. This one may be a stretch for a 40yo NEET but if you get the right gig, or hop into the right gig after getting a year experience, you may have found a cozy job. Many janitors are not running around like rats and barely have to deal with coworkers or the public. Maybe you end up hired by the local school board in half a year and make "decent" money.


If you really want to try your hand at job where you have a taskmaster watching you try these:

Warehouse worker.
Meatpacker.
Day laborer: Construction, landscaping, whatever.
Factory as a production worker. GL getting hired unless the job is literal hell with high turnover.
Outbound Call Centre

Good luck. If you show up every day for a 1 year or 2 a manager or senior coworker will likely give you a glowing reference. You can even become a real "worker" and have easier mornings in time.

62474821
>>62474795
None of those are hiring.

62474824
>>62474821
Then keep whining here like a bitch. Someone else will take the advice and make a few dollars. If a call centre won't take you, you may as well start picking up cans and bottles.

62474838
>>62474824
Just admit that you're wrong. There are no jobs.

62474844
>>62474795
if you're a man you don't exist. don't go to die in a dead end gas station. karma is real.

62474884
>>62449961
Anon, try asking an LLM (I recommend chat gpt but use whatever) on how to get a job. Explain your entire situation in as much detail as possible, why you haven't worked, depression, you're 40, never had a job, where you live (so it can recommend options) that you're carless.

Explain EVERYTHING. Learning how to utilize LLMs will really help you anyway. I'm getting close to 40 myself and I use LLMs daily (I am a professional with a good job not a NEET but it doesn't matter you need to use LLMs now even if you're employed). Anons ITT are mostly going to dunk on you because you're a huge fucking loser but the best time to turn your life around is right the fuck now. The LLM won't make you feel bad like everyone here is doing too.

Explain your interests to it too and maybe it can try and recommend a job you'll actually excel somewhat in, although beggars can't be choosy at this point.

62474892
>>62474884
the creature making the OP does not want advice. It's doing an act for (you)s
This is actually good advice to someone so defeated they can't find jobs felons on the sex offender registry get after 15 years binned up.
I'm sure a free AI could coach a defeated NEET into one of those jobs.
OP isn't though, just a weird fuck

62475051
>>62474884
I think this is the first time I've seen someone call Ai like chat gpt an "LLM". Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.

>>62474892
Get a life you fucking loser. Dogshit like you doesn't deserve even the attention I've given you already.

62475932
>>62475051
>Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.
Repeating myself, but the modern human needs to get comfortable using LLMs to be productive and successful. Avoid falling in to emotional or political traps involving them. View them as a useful tool. A human who refuses to use a calculator would be less productive than a human who does use a calculator.

The same is true for LLMs. Don't blindly trust everything it tells you. Get a feel for how to prompt it to give you the results you want and double check things that you aren't sure about. A lot of people on /biz/ and in general seethe over LLMs, call you a pajeet if you mention them etc, just ignore them. I'm a professional making 6 figs a year in an white collar position, I use LLMs every single day outside of work and inside of work.

As a skill-less NEET who doesn't know how to navigate the world it's the most invaluable tool at your disposal.

62475990
>>62449961
very white european of you

62476030
>>62475932
You put way too much faith in an algorithm poorly masquerading as ai. If it's a tool it should be a learning tool, not a crutch.

62476048

It's genuinely incredible how convinced some of you are that price is the only metric that matters. That's exactly why retail always ends up as exit liquidity. You spend your entire time chasing green candles while ignoring the bigger picture because that's easier than understanding what's happening beneath the surface. Institutions don't operate on Twitter timelines, they operate on regulatory clarity, infrastructure and long-term planning. I've heard every argument imaginable for years. "Ripple is a scam." "The lawsuit killed XRP." "The executives are dumping." "The banks aren't using it." It's always the same recycled talking points from people who've done ten minutes of research before declaring themselves experts. Every time one narrative falls apart, another appears because admitting your thesis was wrong is apparently impossible. The funniest part is watching people who entered crypto during hype cycles lecture long-term holders about conviction. I've watched entire ecosystems explode and disappear while XRP has continued building through uncertainty that would've killed most projects. Somehow surviving a major legal battle gets twisted into a negative because it doesn't fit the narrative. That's some world-class cognitive dissonance. Could I have made more money chasing every meme coin? Maybe. Could I have also blown up my portfolio chasing hype? Absolutely. My thesis was never based on dopamine pumps or influencer engagement. It was based on utility eventually mattering once speculation fades. Call it cope if that makes you feel better. I've heard "just one more year bro" for years and it still doesn't change the fundamentals. Markets have a habit of making patient people look stupid right before rewarding them. Maybe I'm wrong, maybe I'm right, but I'd rather stick to a thesis built over years of research than change my opinion every time a chart moves. I'll happily keep holding my so-called dead bags

62481649
The oil industry goes through cycles. When prices are high, it makes it economically profitable to drill new wells and then everyone starts pumping as much as they can to make as much money as they can. If economic growth is good, this can create a balance between the oil being produced and the growth fueling the economy, but if growth starts to lag or if oil production simply outpaces it, then there starts to be a glut and oil prices drop.

This is where were were at in the cycle prior to the Iran war. When this happens, the cost to run many wells exceeds what they can get from the oil so they have to shut them down. Then the glut gets worked through, prices go back up and the cycle begins anew.

The Iran war has massively distorted the normal cycles. The glut we were supposed to work through slowly and steadily as wells went offline here and there has been burned through. Oil producers got higher prices for a while allowing them to maintain output at profitable levels for a while longer, but the gulf states got fucked. They will have to start capping wells because they have nowhere to put their oil seeing as how it can't get out of the strait.

Strategic reserves will have to be replenished after this, so prices will remain high as government replenish their reserves.

The one unanswered question here is how much of the glut prior to all this came from a faltering economy. If the economy was already bad, then having the glut burned through quickly combined with strategic reserves buying, the price of oil will be too high to support the economic recovery that will be needed. The fact that the price keeps going down again no matter the supply shocks suggests there is actually demand destruction on a massive scale, probably because China is having a recession. That will drag down the global economy into a recession if it hasn't already.

item.title: Why is there so much negativity surrounding the DRK? — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is there so much negativity surrounding the DRK?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-15 08:21 UTC

OP

Every time I see the DRK mentioned here, it is always in a negative light.

It is always
>In the USA, wages are better!
>In Europe, houses are cheaper!
>The economy is underperforming!
>My taxes are too high!

There is never any mention of the fantastic benefits which come with life in the best Korea. The fantastic healthcare for minimal cost, the great education system available to everybody, the dynamic modern economy with many bustling sectors, and everything else.

Taxes are higher than the USA but they are lower than most of Europe and we still enjoy fantastic public services whereas in America there are no public services or safety nets.

Not to mention the things you cannot measure. Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs. Enjoying the fantastic beautiful nature and gardens. Living in the historical majesty and balancing that with modern cosmopolitan qualities.

The DRK might not be the best place to become rich but nobody is poor here and it is very easy to enjoy life if you have goals beyond the number in your bank account.

The Clarity Act is important because it would provide a much clearer regulatory framework for cryptocurrency and establish clearer rules for the industry. That legal certainty is one of the biggest reasons many financial institutions have been cautious about fully embracing blockchain technology and tokenizing real-world assets. Especially since you see so many scams going on, and those influencers never see the inside of a jail cell. It makes it harder for businesses and banks to take crypto seriously and makes them hesitant because if the SEC decides to act against them, there are no clear rules to protect them from liability. Companies managing billions of dollars aren’t going to want to invest in new tech because, again, there are no clear rules. If the act passes then more institutions will feel comfortable using blockchain technology.

>>62482182
also eventually Ethereum will grow so large that only corporate datacenters will be able to run nodes and they will basically just shut it down afterwards

62482702
>>62482182
You can already upload pictures. Calldata costs 16 gas per byte so calculating from that your 440 KB picrel would cost around $1.50.
>>62482185
It's growing pretty fast right now but there's changes like state expiry and stateless nodes coming. https://ethereum.org/roadmap/statelessness Even if they're delayed for years the hardware cost for a node will only go up like 50 dollars per year (~500GB per year, one time change from 2TB recommended minimum to 4TB) which is far from "only corporate datacenter".
